title: MCP server not starting on Emacs startup
description:
Root cause: elpaca-use-package-mode defers package loading. The :config block's (add-hook 'emacs-startup-hook ...) never ran during init because mcp-server only loaded on first autoload. By the time the user manually called mcp-server-start-unix, emacs-startup-hook had already fired.

Fix: Added :demand t. Changed from :config to :hook (emacs-startup . mcp-server-start-unix).
close_reason: MCP server starts on Emacs startup — :demand t + :init hook fix is working

title: MCP server config for Emacs
description: Adding rhblind/emacs-mcp-server to Emacs config so opencode can control Emacs via MCP tools. Files: lisp/my-mcp.el (recipe + auto-start), init.el:74 (require). Pending: restart Emacs, register with opencode.
close_reason: MCP server tested and working. Socket at ~/.emacs.d/emacs-mcp-server.sock with 12 tools available.

title: Optimize lisp/ directory structure and startup
description:
Completed: subdirectory restructuring (lisp/{config,tools,langs,org}). my-rust kept and loaded. my-coffee and my-lua left on disk but not loaded.

Remaining:
- elpaca-wait cleanup — remove excess elpaca-wait calls from init.el
- Split my-env.el — 287 lines, grab-bag
- Language mode autoloading — use :mode instead of eager require

comments.author: Paul C. Mantz
comments.text:
2026-05-24: Completed lisp/ directory restructuring

- Created subdirectories: lisp/{config,tools,langs,org}
- Moved files to appropriate subdirectories with git mv
- Split my-major-modes.el into lisp/tools/my-vc.el (magit, transient, diff-hl, beads) and lisp/langs/my-misc-major-modes.el (web-mode, plantuml, sh-script, php-mode)
- Renamed my-data-modes.el -> my-misc-data-modes.el (catch-all for data format modes)
- Updated init.el with new load paths and requires
- Moved comint/shell/ansi-color config from my-env.el and my-major-modes.el into my-terminal.el
- Removed dead projectile-multi-term-in-root reference to uninstalled multi-term package

title: chezmoi-manage Emacs config
description: Move ~/.emacs.d symlink management into chezmoi: set up ~/.local/share/chezmoi/ to track /Users/pcmantz/git/elisp with proper symlink. This came up during MCP server work.
notes: Windows note: Emacs does NOT follow XDG on Windows. HOME defaults to AppData\Roaming (Vista+) or Application Data (XP), then ~/.emacs.d/. No XDG check for ~/.config/emacs/. Workaround: set /Users/pcmantz explicitly or use --init-directory (Emacs 29+).

comments.author: Paul C. Mantz
comments.text:
## MCP Server Architecture (discovered 2026-05-24)

Two Emacs sessions are running:
1. **Interactive Emacs** (PID 32034) — GUI with Magit, Dired, Org, vterm. User's main session.
2. **Batch Emacs** (PID 34159, now killed) — launched via `--batch` with explicit load paths (not `init.el`), PPID 1 (launchd), runs `(mcp-server-start-unix)` then loops forever. Served the Unix socket at `~/.emacs.d/emacs-mcp-server.sock`.

The user's `my-mcp.el` also calls `(mcp-server-start-unix)` in the interactive Emacs, but the socket was already held by the batch process.

### MCP Connection Chain
opencode (vterm) -> socat -> Batch Emacs (mcp-server)

### Config Files
- `~/.config/opencode/opencode.jsonc`: socat bridges stdio to socket
- `~/.emacs.d/lisp/my-mcp.el`: calls `(mcp-server-start-unix)`
- `~/.emacs.d/init.el`: line 74 loads `my-mcp`

### Action Taken
- Killed batch Emacs (PID 34159) and socat (PID 35614)
- Removed stale socket file
- Next: start `(mcp-server-start-unix)` from interactive Emacs, then reconnect opencode
